description: A file system server providing comprehensive operations for reading, writing, editing, and managing files and directories, including metadata retrieval and recursive search capabilities.

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read the complete contents of a text file, with optional line filtering to retrieve only the first or last N lines.

path.description: The absolute or relative file path to the text file to read.

head.description: Optional number of lines to read from the beginning of the file.

tail.description: Optional number of lines to read from the end of the file.

file_content.description: The contents of the file decoded as UTF-8 text.

tools.name: read_media_file
tools.description: Read a binary media file (image or audio) and return its contents as base64-encoded data with MIME type information.

path.description: The absolute or relative file path to the media file to read.

data.description: The base64-encoded binary content of the media file.

mime_type.description: The MIME type of the media file (e.g., 'image/png', 'audio/mp3').

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Read multiple files simultaneously and return their contents or any errors encountered for each file.

paths.description: A list of absolute or relative file paths to read.

path.description: The file path that was read.

content.description: The contents of the file if successfully read.

error.description: Error message if the file could not be read.

results.description: A list of results for each requested file, containing either content or an error message.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Create a new file or overwrite an existing file with the specified content.

path.description: The absolute or relative file path where the content will be written.

content.description: The text content to write to the file.

path.description: The file path where the content was written.

success.description: A message indicating the write operation completed successfully.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Make selective edits to a file using advanced pattern matching, with support for dry-run previews before applying changes.

path.description: The absolute or relative file path to the file to edit.

oldText.description: Optional text pattern to search for within the file (can be a substring).

newText.description: Optional replacement text to substitute for matched occurrences.

dryRun.description: If true, preview the changes without applying them to the file. Defaults to false.

diff.description: A unified diff showing the changes made or to be made to the file.

line_number.description: The line number where the match was found.

context.description: The surrounding context of the matched text.

matches.description: A list of matches found, including line numbers and surrounding context.

applied.description: Indicates whether the changes were actually applied to the file.

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a new directory at the specified path, or ensure the directory already exists.

path.description: The absolute or relative path of the directory to create.
The path of the directory that was created or already existed.

created.description: True if a new directory was created, false if it already existed.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List the contents of a directory, returning each entry with its name, type, and size.

path.description: The absolute or relative path of the directory to list.

name.description: The name of the file or directory entry.

type.description: The type of entry: 'FILE' for files or 'DIR' for directories.

size.description: The size of the entry in bytes.

entries.description: A list of directory entries with their names, types, and sizes.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename a file or directory from a source path to a destination path.

source.description: The absolute or relative path of the source file or directory to move.

destination.description: The absolute or relative path of the destination location.

source.description: The original path of the moved file or directory.

destination.description: The new path of the moved file or directory.

success.description: Indicates whether the move operation completed successfully.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Recursively search for files and directories matching a glob pattern, with optional exclusion patterns.

path.description: The absolute or relative path of the starting directory for the search.

pattern.description: A glob-style pattern to match file and directory names (e.g., '*.txt', '**/*.py').

excludePatterns.description: Optional list of glob-style patterns to exclude from search results.

matches.description: A list of full paths to files and directories matching the search pattern.

tools.name: directory_tree
tools.description: Generate a recursive JSON tree structure representing the contents of a directory and all its subdirectories.

path.description: The absolute or relative path of the starting directory for the tree.

excludePatterns.description: Optional list of glob-style patterns to exclude from the tree structure.

name.description: The name of the file or directory.

children.description: Nested entries for directories; present only for directory type entries.

tree.description: A hierarchical tree structure representing the directory contents.

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file or directory, including size, timestamps, and permissions.

path.description: The absolute or relative path of the file or directory to inspect.
The path of the inspected file or directory.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes, or total size for directories.

created_time.description: The creation timestamp of the file or directory in ISO 8601 format.

modified_time.description: The last modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

access_time.description: The last access timestamp in ISO 8601 format.

permissions.description: The file system permissions in symbolic notation (e.g., 'rwxr-xr-x').
